findings.title: Description mismatch between marketplace.json and plugin.json
findings.snippet: "description": "Comprehensive codebase analysis across 4 standard dimensions (structure, quality, security, testing) — produces scored reports with critic validation and focused refactoring plans. v0.8.0 adds token optimizations (split schemas, skip-clean ralph-loop passes, inline scoring, smart model defaults)."
findings.description: The marketplace.json description ('Multi-dimensional codebase analysis across 4 standard dimensions (structure, quality, security, testing) with plugin-specific analysis mode, scored reports, critic validation, and refactoring plan generation') differs from plugin.json ('Comprehensive codebase analysis across 4 standard dimensions...v0.8.0 adds token optimizations...'). The marketplace entry uses 'Multi-dimensional' while plugin.json uses 'Comprehensive', and plugin.json includes v0.8.0 changelog details that the marketplace entry omits. Additionally, the marketplace entry mentions 'plugin-specific analysis mode' which the plugin.json description does not.
findings.recommendation: Synchronize descriptions: update marketplace.json to match the current plugin.json description, or craft a shared canonical description used in both places. Remove version-specific changelog details from the description field (those belong in the changelog).

findings.title: Tags present in plugin.json but absent in marketplace entry
findings.description: plugin.json declares 9 keywords (dev, analysis, refactoring, code-quality, structure, security, testing, scoring, plugin-analysis) but the marketplace entry has no tags or keywords field. While not required by the marketplace schema, aligning keywords improves discoverability.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a 'tags' array to the marketplace entry mirroring the plugin.json keywords, or accept the divergence as intentional.
